jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but not original within the show's context

jokes.text: I mean, half these bottles ain't even mine.
jokes.setup: Community service for cleaning up litter
jokes.punchline: Barney implies he's responsible for the other half
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor that reveals Barney's drinking problem through implication rather than stating it directly

jokes.text: Oh, no! He's going over the falls!
jokes.setup: Homer appears to be in mortal danger
jokes.punchline: Beginning of escalating comedy sequence
jokes.explanation: Sets up brilliant escalating sequence - strong structure but could be delivered by any character

jokes.text: Oh, good. He snagged that tree branch. Oh, no! The branch broke off.
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal of fortune
jokes.explanation: Classic yo-yo structure of hope and despair, economically executed

jokes.text: Oh, good. Those helpful beavers are swimming out to save him. Oh, no. They're biting him and stealing his pants.
jokes.punchline: Beavers are actually making things worse in ridiculous way
jokes.explanation: Peak of escalating sequence - 'stealing his pants' is perfectly absurd detail that pushes beyond expected violence into pure cartoon logic

jokes.text: I'll cross him off the list.
jokes.setup: Smithers delivers eulogy about Homer being one of the finest, bravest men
jokes.punchline: Burns treats it as simple administrative task
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - cold efficiency in face of death, excellent economy and structure

jokes.text: That dummy worked like a charm, Dad. Best 600 bucks I ever spent.
jokes.punchline: Reveals entire sequence was elaborate fake death scheme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that recontextualizes entire opening sequence - high craft in structure and earned surprise

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I-I'm just nervous. I didn't mean any disrespect.
jokes.setup: Flanders realizes his quirky speech was inappropriate
jokes.punchline: His awkward over-apologizing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Flanders' social anxiety, but not particularly memorable or quotable

jokes.text: He's alive in all our hearts.
jokes.setup: Marge says Homer is alive in the backyard
jokes.punchline: Flanders thinks she's in denial, offers platitude
jokes.explanation: Standard grief platitude humor - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Your electricity's in the name of HomerJ. Simpson, deceased. The juice stays off till you get a job or a generator.
jokes.setup: Homer's fake death creates bureaucratic problems
jokes.punchline: Absurd catch-22 - dead person needs job to get electricity
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic absurdism - the logic is perfectly backwards in a realistic way

jokes.text: I don't like your attitude, you watercooler dictator.
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific insult combining office setting with authority figure
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that fits Homer's confrontational style and the office setting

jokes.text: Aha! See? This thing is all screwed up! Who the heck is Margaret Simpson?
jokes.setup: Homer reading his file to prove government incompetence
jokes.punchline: He doesn't recognize his own daughter's real name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - he's so disconnected from his family he doesn't know Maggie's real name

jokes.text: Leaves of Grass my ass!
jokes.setup: Homer discovers Walt Whitman's grave instead of his mother's
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude dismissal of classic literature
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer's anti-intellectual stance delivered with perfect crudeness

jokes.text: Why does my death keep coming back to haunt me?
jokes.setup: Homer finds his own grave marker
jokes.punchline: Reverses normal phrase - living person haunted by own death
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of common phrase - typically the dead haunt the living, not the other way around

jokes.text: You awful, awful man! Get out of my son's grave!
jokes.setup: Strange woman attacks Homer at grave
jokes.punchline: She claims it's her son's grave
jokes.explanation: Sets up the reveal but not particularly funny on its own - more plot development

jokes.text: Well, dang blast it! Isn't anybody in this dad-gummed cemetery dead?
jokes.setup: Two presumed dead people meet at cemetery
jokes.punchline: Groundskeeper's exasperated observation about cemetery's failure at its basic function
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor - perfectly captures the absurdity of the situation from a working man's perspective

jokes.text: I didn't want to cause a fuss, but now that you mention it.
jokes.setup: Groundskeeper asked if anyone is dead
jokes.punchline: Deadpan acknowledgment that he himself is dead
jokes.explanation: Exceptional deadpan delivery - perfect economy and the polite understatement about being dead is brilliantly absurd

jokes.text: Some people say I look like Dan Aykroyd.
jokes.setup: Mona says Homer grew up handsome
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional comparison to Dan Aykroyd
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's delusions about his appearance - Dan Aykroyd is a specific, unglamorous choice

jokes.text: I almost always spoil the moment.
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware admission that he's about to ruin it
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware character moment - Homer knows his own flaws and warns about them, which is both funny and touching

jokes.text: It's like something out of Dickens. Or Melrose Place.
jokes.punchline: Follows high culture reference with trashy soap opera
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Lisa's mix of intelligence and being influenced by pop culture

jokes.text: You owe me... $22,000. I'll Kwanzaa you.!
jokes.setup: Bart demands back-payment for missed holidays
jokes.punchline: Grandma threatens violence using Kwanzaa as a verb
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay turning holiday name into threat - shows grandma can match Bart's attitude

jokes.text: I'll Kwanzaa you.!
jokes.setup: Bart demanding money for missing Kwanzaas
jokes.punchline: Mona threatening to 'Kwanzaa' him as retaliation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning holiday name into a threat

jokes.text: Yeah, more like preschool. I hateJohn Knowles.
jokes.setup: Discussion of reading level and literature
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual hatred of respected author
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's anti-intellectual stance, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: You didn't dumb it down. You said 'rapport.'
jokes.punchline: She's excited that someone uses vocabulary words with her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - she's so starved for intellectual conversation that basic vocabulary thrills her

jokes.text: These are the calling cards of a con artist.
jokes.setup: Lisa finds multiple IDs in grandmother's purse
jokes.punchline: She immediately jumps to criminal conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's detective instincts and vocabulary

jokes.text: First, it wasn't 25 years. It was 27 years.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests Homer could get hurt by mother who abandoned him for 25 years
jokes.punchline: Homer corrects the timeframe while missing the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - focuses on irrelevant detail while completely missing emotional point

jokes.text: Spill it, Muddy Mae, or we're calling the cops!
jokes.punchline: Uses one of her fake names like he's a detective
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the fake IDs, shows Bart taking charge of interrogation

jokes.text: Darn tootin'!
jokes.punchline: Child Homer using old-fashioned exclamation
jokes.explanation: Mild period humor - child using old-fashioned expression fits the 1960s setting

jokes.text: Joe Willie Namath swaggering off the field... his sideburns an apogee of sculpted 'sartorium'- the foppish follicles pioneered by Ambrose Burnside... Appomattox, 1865.
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate historical analysis of sideburn evolution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa Simpson - connects modern football to Civil War through absurdly academic analysis of facial hair

jokes.text: Look at them sideburns! He looks like a girl. Now, Johnny Unitas- there's a haircut you could set your watch to.
jokes.punchline: Prefers extremely conservative quarterback's look
jokes.explanation: Classic old man humor contrasting flashy 60s style with conservative values - 'set your watch to' is perfect period language

jokes.text: Ptooh their flower power- it's no match for my glower power.
jokes.punchline: Creates rhyming counter-slogan about his intimidating glare
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Burns' old-fashioned villainy and his pride in being menacing

jokes.text: My germs! My precious germs! They never harmed a soul. They never even had a chance.
jokes.punchline: Treats deadly germs like beloved pets
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - treats instruments of mass destruction with parental affection

jokes.text: Listen to me breathe.
jokes.setup: Burns realizes his asthma is cured
jokes.punchline: Asks someone to listen to his breathing as if it's impressive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' narcissism - treats basic bodily function as achievement to be admired

jokes.text: I ran credit checks at Tom Hayden's Porsche dealership.
jokes.setup: Mona describing how radical friends helped her survive
jokes.punchline: Former radical activist now sells luxury cars
jokes.explanation: Good satire of 60s radicals selling out - Tom Hayden the revolutionary now as car salesman is perfect irony

jokes.text: Yes, I'd like to send this letter to the Prussian consulate in Siam by aeromail. Am I too late for the 4:30 autogiro?
jokes.punchline: Otto requests service to countries/transportation that don't exist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto moment - combines his drug-addled confusion with period-appropriate archaic references

jokes.text: She has the sloping brow and cranial bumpage of the career criminal.
jokes.punchline: Uses discredited phrenology to diagnose criminality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns anachronism - using 19th century pseudoscience, plus 'cranial bumpage' is wonderfully specific

jokes.text: Of course you'd say that. You have the brainpan of a stagecoach tilter.
jokes.punchline: Burns doubles down with even more archaic insult
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Burns insult - 'stagecoach tilter' is brilliantly archaic and specific, perfect character voice and economy

jokes.text: Gee, Joe. You haven't been the same since your son went crazy in Vietnam.
jokes.setup: Partner makes philosophical comment about idealism
jokes.punchline: Reveals personal trauma behind the philosophy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Vietnam War trauma - functional but not particularly distinctive

jokes.text: Seven! No, Dad. It's a rhetorical question. Rhetorical, eh? Eight!
jokes.punchline: Grandpa treats 'rhetorical' as another question to answer with a number
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa Simpson moment - his senile literal-mindedness creates absurd escalation

jokes.text: Oh, Abe! You've aged terribly.
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of his appearance after 27 years
jokes.explanation: Direct insult humor - economical but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Can we have sex? Please?
jokes.punchline: Abe interrupts drama with inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Perfect tension-breaking moment - Abe's hopeful desperation cuts through dramatic scene

jokes.text: Yeah, I seen her. Uh, that is to say, I saw her.
jokes.punchline: Self-corrects grammar while talking to authorities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Apu's education and desire to speak properly

jokes.text: Look at me, Grandma. I'm a hippie. Peace, man. Groovy. Bomb Vietnam! Four more years! Up with people!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to connect with his grandmother's radical past
jokes.punchline: Mixing contradictory political slogans
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's lack of political understanding

jokes.text: We're only now finally caught up.
jokes.setup: Agent says biological warfare was set back 30 years
jokes.punchline: Implies they're back to developing dangerous weapons
jokes.explanation: Dark government humor but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Put out an A.P.B. on a Uosdwis R. Dewoh. Uh, better start with Greek town. That's HomerJ. Simpson, Chief. You're reading it upside down.
jokes.setup: Chief reading name from paper napkin
jokes.punchline: Reading 'Homer J Simpson' upside down creates nonsense name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag that works in audio - perfect Wiggum incompetence with clever wordplay element

jokes.text: Uh, cancel that A.P.B. But, uh- Oh, bring back some of them, uh, gyros.
jokes.punchline: Still wants Greek food despite canceling Greek town search
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Wiggum's food-focused priorities even during police work

jokes.text: Uh, Chief, you're talking into your wallet.
jokes.punchline: He's confused his radio with his wallet
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy showing Wiggum's absent-mindedness

jokes.text: All right, I admit it. I am the Lindbergh baby. Waah! Waah! Goo, goo. I miss my fly-fly dada.
jokes.punchline: Abe confesses to being famous kidnapping victim while doing baby talk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist confession - historically impossible claim delivered with perfect baby voice adds to the surreal humor

jokes.text: Are you trying to stall us, or are you just senile? A little from column 'A,' a little from column 'B.'
jokes.punchline: Abe admits to both intentional and unintentional confusion
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where Abe admits his strategic use of senility - honest character insight

jokes.text: Well, it's 'cause of your old lady that I got rid of the asthma... that was keeping me out of the academy.
jokes.setup: Wiggum reveals he's the anonymous tipster
jokes.punchline: Burns' germ lab destruction accidentally helped future police chief
jokes.explanation: Great callback connecting plot threads - Mona's activism accidentally created incompetent police chief

jokes.text: Hurry up, man.! This electric van only has 20 minutes of juice left!
jokes.punchline: Underground resistance uses environmentally conscious but impractical vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good period satire - 1990s environmental consciousness applied to dramatic escape

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase ends the episode
jokes.explanation: Bookends episode nicely but not particularly creative usage of the catchphrase
